name_cn: TOU Tariff-driven Demand Side Response Scenario
name_en: TOU Tariff-driven Demand Side Response Scenario
description_cn: Uses time-of-use tariff signals to guide flexible user loads for active peak shaving and valley filling, smoothing net load while reducing losses and improving operating economics.
description_en: Simulates responsive flexible loads shifting demand according to Time-of-Use tariff to achieve peak shaving and optimization.
typical_user_requests: Add flexible loads for peak shaving
Evaluate demand response impacts on the grid

price_elasticity.description: Price elasticity coefficient of load demand.

validation_rules.message_cn: The configured adjustable flexible-load ratio exceeds 30%, which is difficult to achieve in engineering practice. Confirm that this is intended.
A flat tariff conflicts with the semantics of a TOU-tariff-driven demand response scenario.

result_interpretation_focus: Analyze load shifting between high- and low-price periods to determine whether demand response achieves effective peak shaving and valley filling.
Evaluate reductions in electricity purchase cost and total grid loss to measure overall control benefits.
Observe whether voltage compliance improves during peak periods due to load reduction.
